### Medical Tourism: India vs Thailand vs Turkey — 2026 Comparison Guide
Compare medical tourism in India, Thailand, and Turkey for 2026. Cost comparison tables by procedure, quality benchmarks, visa ease, and which destination is best for your treatment.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/medical-tourism-india-vs-thailand-turkey
Category: Country Guides | Date: 2026-07-20 | Read time: 8 min | Author: Heal India Team

Choosing the right country for your medical treatment abroad is as important as choosing the right surgeon. Three destinations dominate global medical tourism: India, Thailand, and Turkey. Each has a strong value proposition, but they serve different patient profiles, procedure types, and budgets.

This guide provides an honest, data-driven comparison to help you decide.

---

## At a Glance: How the Three Destinations Compare

| Factor | India | Thailand | Turkey |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Best for** | Complex procedures, high-cost savings, holistic recovery | Cosmetic surgery, wellness retreats, fertility | Hair transplants, cosmetic dentistry, rhinoplasty |
| **JCI hospitals** | 41 (most in Asia) | 62 | 10 |
| **Cost vs Western** | 60–90% less | 40–70% less | 50–75% less |
| **Visa ease** | e-Medical Visa (3 days) | Medical visa (1–2 weeks) | e-Visa (24 hours) |
| **Recovery options** | Ayurveda, yoga, Kerala backwaters | Spa resorts, island recovery | Thermal hotels, Cappadocia tours |
| **Languages** | English widely spoken | English in tourist areas | English in medical centres |
| **Flight time (from UK)** | 9–11 hours | 11–13 hours | 4–5 hours |
| **Number of patients/year** | 7.3 million | 3.5 million | 1.5 million |

---

## Cost Comparison by Procedure

| Procedure | India (USD) | Thailand (USD) | Turkey (USD) | US Cost (Reference) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Heart Bypass (CABG) | $5,000–$8,500 | $15,000–$22,000 | $12,000–$20,000 | $90,000–$150,000 |
| Knee Replacement | $3,500–$7,500 | $10,000–$15,000 | $8,000–$14,000 | $35,000–$49,000 |
| Hip Replacement | $3,000–$7,200 | $10,000–$15,000 | $8,000–$13,000 | $35,000–$50,000 |
| IVF Cycle | $2,500–$5,000 | $6,000–$10,000 | $4,000–$8,000 | $18,000–$24,000 |
| Rhinoplasty | $1,200–$2,500 | $3,000–$6,000 | $2,000–$4,500 | $5,000–$15,000 |
| Dental Implant (single) | $600–$900 | $1,500–$3,000 | $400–$800 | $3,000–$5,000 |
| Hair Transplant (FUE, 2000 grafts) | $1,500–$3,000 | $3,000–$6,000 | $1,500–$2,500 | $8,000–$20,000 |

---

## Deep Dive: When to Choose Each Destination

### Choose India If...

**You need complex, high-acuity surgery.** India's strength is in cardiac surgery, orthopedics, oncology, organ transplantation, and neurosurgery. With 41 JCI-accredited hospitals and surgeons who perform 400–500 cardiac procedures annually, India offers depth of expertise that Thailand and Turkey don't match in these categories.

**Cost savings matter most.** India is consistently 20–40% cheaper than Thailand and 15–30% cheaper than Turkey for the same procedure — and 70–90% cheaper than US prices.

**You want a holistic recovery.** Only India offers post-surgery Ayurvedic rehabilitation. The combination of advanced surgery at a JCI hospital followed by 2–3 weeks of Ayurvedic recovery in Kerala is a value proposition no other destination can match.

### Choose Thailand If...

**Cosmetic surgery or wellness is your priority.** Thailand has a strong reputation for cosmetic procedures and luxury recovery experiences. The recovery environment — beach resorts, spa retreats — is hard to beat.

**You prefer a tourist-friendly experience.** Thailand's tourism infrastructure is more developed than India's or Turkey's. If your treatment is straightforward and your priority is a vacation-like experience, Thailand excels.

**Distance from Asia-Pacific matters.** For patients from Australia, New Zealand, Japan, or Southeast Asia, Thailand is significantly closer than India or Turkey.

### Choose Turkey If...

**Hair transplant or cosmetic dentistry is your goal.** Turkey has the highest volume of hair transplant procedures globally and extremely competitive pricing for dental work.

**Proximity to Europe matters.** At 4–5 hours from London and 3 hours from Berlin, Turkey is the most accessible destination for European patients.

**You prefer a short stay.** Many cosmetic procedures in Turkey are designed for 3–5 day stays. India typically requires longer minimum stays (2–4 weeks for complex procedures).

---

## Quality Comparison: Accreditation & Outcomes

| Metric | India | Thailand | Turkey |
|---|---|---|---|
| JCI-accredited hospitals | 41 | 62 | 10 |
| NABH/SHA accredited | 700+ | — | — |
| CABG success rate | 98–99% | 97–99% | 97–99% |
| IVF success (under 35) | 50–60% | 50–60% | 45–55% |
| Infection rate (accredited) | 1–2% | 1–2% | 1–3% |

All three countries maintain excellent clinical outcomes at accredited facilities. The key is choosing the right hospital and surgeon — not just the country.

---

## Visa & Travel Comparison

| | India | Thailand | Turkey |
|---|---|---|---|
| Medical visa type | e-Medical Visa | Medical visa (TR) | e-Visa (medical) |
| Processing time | 24–72 hours | 1–2 weeks | 24 hours |
| Visa cost | ~$80 | ~$60 | ~$50 |
| Max stay | 60 days (extendable) | 90 days | 90 days |
| Companion visa | e-Medical Attendant | Tourist visa | e-Visa (companion) |

---

## Our Recommendation

| If you need... | Best destination |
|---|---|
| Heart surgery, cancer treatment, organ transplant | **India** |
| Knee/hip replacement | **India** (best value) or **Thailand** (luxury recovery) |
| IVF / fertility | **India** (best cost per cycle) |
| Rhinoplasty / cosmetic surgery | **Turkey** or **Thailand** |
| Hair transplant | **Turkey** |
| Dental implants | **Turkey** (cost) or **India** (Kerala + holiday) |
| Post-surgery holistic recovery | **India** (Ayurveda, Kerala) |

### Ayush Prana: Post-Surgery Ayurveda Recovery in Kerala — Complete Guide
Combine advanced surgery with Ayurvedic recovery at Ayush Prana, Kerala. Learn about post-surgery rehabilitation, costs, and how it accelerates healing.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/ayush-prana-post-surgery-ayurveda-recovery
Category: Ayurveda & Wellness | Date: 2026-07-18 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

You've chosen to have surgery in India — a decision driven by world-class hospitals, expert surgeons, and significant cost savings. But there's another reason patients from 50+ countries choose Kerala for their medical journey: **post-surgery Ayurvedic recovery.**

Ayush Prana, the world's only Ayurvedic Multiple Sclerosis specialty hospital located on the banks of the Periyar River near Kochi, has extended its expertise to post-surgery rehabilitation — helping patients recover faster, with less pain, and better long-term outcomes than standard recovery protocols alone.

---

## Why Ayurvedic Recovery After Surgery?

Surgery, no matter how minimally invasive, is traumatic to the body. Anaesthesia, tissue manipulation, inflammation, and the stress of travel all take a toll. Standard post-operative care focuses on pain management and basic physiotherapy. Ayurvedic recovery adds several powerful dimensions:

| Recovery Factor | Standard Approach | Ayurvedic Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Inflammation | NSAIDs (anti-inflammatory drugs) | Herbal formulations + external therapies |
| Tissue healing | Time + basic nutrition | Targeted herbal preparations + detoxification |
| Pain management | Opioids / NSAIDs | Herbal oils, medicated steam, gentle massage |
| Energy recovery | Rest only | Rasayana (rejuvenation) therapies |
| Long-term immunity | Not addressed | Immunomodulatory herbs |
| Stress reduction | Minimal | Meditation, yoga, counselling |

---

## The Ayush Prana Advantage

Located just 25 km from Kochi International Airport, Ayush Prana sits on the banks of the Periyar River surrounded by forest. This is not a hotel-style spa — it is a dedicated Ayurvedic treatment centre with over two decades of experience and an in-house GMP-certified pharmacy.

### What the Post-Surgery Program Includes

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| Initial assessment | Ayurvedic physician consultation, pulse diagnosis (Nadi Pariksha) |
| Customised detox (Panchakarma) | Gentle detox protocols appropriate for post-surgery patients |
| Herbal medications | Custom formulations from in-house pharmacy, adjusted weekly |
| External therapies | Medicated oil massage (Abhyanga), steam therapy (Swedana), herbal poultices |
| Physiotherapy integration | Coordinated with your surgical team's rehabilitation plan |
| Dietary planning | Personalised meals to reduce inflammation and accelerate tissue repair |
| Yoga and meditation | Gentle practices adapted to your surgical recovery stage |
| Duration | 7–28 days depending on procedure and recovery progress |

### Typical Recovery Timeline by Procedure

| Procedure | Hospital Stay | Recommended Ayush Prana Stay | Total Time in India |
|---|---|---|---|
| Knee/Hip Replacement | 4–5 days | 14–21 days | 3–4 weeks |
| Heart Bypass (CABG) | 5–7 days | 14–21 days | 3–4 weeks |
| Spine Surgery | 3–5 days | 14–21 days | 3–4 weeks |
| Cosmetic Surgery | 1–3 days | 7–14 days | 2–3 weeks |
| IVF (post-transfer) | Day procedure | 5–7 days | 2–3 weeks |

---

## Cost of Post-Surgery Ayurvedic Recovery

| Program | Duration | Cost (USD) |
|---|---|---|
| Post-surgery recovery (basic) | 7 days | $1,200–$1,800 |
| Post-surgery recovery (comprehensive) | 14 days | $2,200–$3,500 |
| Post-surgery recovery (extended) | 21 days | $3,200–$5,000 |
| Online follow-up consultation | Per session | $50–$100 |

Costs include all therapies, medications, meals, and accommodation. Airport transfers can be arranged.

---

## Who Benefits Most from Post-Surgery Ayurvedic Recovery?

- **Orthopaedic patients** — Reduced stiffness, faster return to mobility, improved implant integration
- **Cardiac patients** — Better circulation, reduced recovery fatigue, improved heart rate variability
- **Spine surgery patients** — Decreased nerve inflammation, improved core strength
- **Cosmetic surgery patients** — Faster wound healing, reduced scarring, improved lymphatic drainage
- **Fertility patients** — Reduced stress, improved uterine blood flow, better implantation outcomes

---

## Patient Story: John's Recovery After Bypass Surgery

*John, 58, from Manchester chose heart bypass surgery at Aster Medcity Kochi. After 6 days in the hospital, he transferred to Ayush Prana for 2 weeks of Ayurvedic recovery.*

*"I expected the surgery to be the hard part. What surprised me was how much the recovery mattered. At Ayush Prana, they didn't just let me rest — they actively helped my body heal. The medicated oil massages, the herbal teas, the gentle yoga. After two weeks, I was walking 2 km a day. My surgeon said my recovery was faster than 90% of his patients."*

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: When can I start Ayurvedic treatment after surgery?**
A: Most patients transfer to Ayush Prana immediately after discharge from the hospital (usually day 4–7 post-surgery).

**Q: Do I need a referral from my surgeon?**
A: Yes. We coordinate with your surgical team to ensure the Ayurvedic protocol complements your medical recovery plan.

**Q: Can my family stay with me?**
A: Yes. Companion accommodation is available.

**Q: Is the treatment safe if I'm on blood thinners or other medications?**
A: Yes. Your Ayurvedic physician works closely with your surgical team. All therapies are adjusted for your specific medications.

**Q: How do I continue the benefits after returning home?**
A: You receive a customised home protocol — dietary guidelines, herbal supplements, and exercise recommendations. Remote follow-up consultations are included.

### Why Thousands of Europeans Are Choosing India for Surgery in 2026
Discover why UK, German, French & other European patients are choosing India for surgery — cost savings of 60-80%, no waiting lists, JCI hospitals, and Ayurveda recovery.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/why-europeans-choose-india-surgery
Category: Country Guides | Date: 2026-07-15 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

The equation is simple: European healthcare systems — whether public (NHS, public health insurance) or private — are struggling with rising costs, growing waiting lists, and limited access to certain procedures. Meanwhile, India's top hospitals offer JCI-accredited care at 60–80% less than European private healthcare prices.

In 2025 alone, over 15,000 patients from the UK and Europe traveled to India for medical treatment — and that number is growing at 18–22% year over year. Here's why.

---

## The Three Drivers Behind the Shift

### 1. Cost Savings That Change Lives

| Procedure | UK Private Cost | India Cost (Kochi) | Savings |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hip Replacement | £15,000–£20,000 | **£3,000–£5,500** | 70–80% |
| Knee Replacement | £14,000–£18,000 | **£2,800–£5,200** | 70–80% |
| Heart Bypass (CABG) | £25,000–£40,000 | **£4,000–£6,500** | 75–85% |
| IVF Cycle | £8,000–£12,000 | **£2,000–£4,000** | 65–75% |
| Rhinoplasty | £5,000–£9,000 | **£1,000–£2,000** | 75–85% |
| Dental Implants (single) | £2,500–£3,500 | **£500–£700** | 75–85% |

Even after adding flights (£500–£800), accommodation (£600–£1,200 for 2–3 weeks), and meals, the total savings still range from **60–80%** compared to UK private care.

### 2. No Waiting Lists

The NHS is struggling with record waiting times:

| Procedure | NHS Wait Time | India Wait Time |
|---|---|---|
| Hip Replacement | 12–18 months | 1–2 weeks |
| Knee Replacement | 14–20 months | 1–2 weeks |
| Heart Bypass | 6–9 months | 3–7 days |
| Cataract Surgery | 8–14 months | 2–3 days |

For patients in pain or with progressive conditions, waiting a year or more isn't just inconvenient — it's detrimental to their health and quality of life. India offers treatment within days of arrival.

### 3. Quality That Matches or Exceeds European Standards

Every hospital in our network holds **JCI (Joint Commission International)** accreditation — the same gold standard required for US hospitals. Many Indian surgeons completed fellowships at Mayo Clinic, Johns Hopkins, King's College London, or Charité Berlin before returning to practice in India.

The clinical outcomes speak for themselves:

- **Cardiac surgery:** 98.6% success rate for CABG at Apollo Hospitals
- **Knee replacement:** 95%+ patient satisfaction at 5-year follow-up
- **IVF:** 50–60% per-cycle success rate for women under 35
- **Cancer care:** Survival outcomes matching leading European centers for common cancers

---

## The Unique Kerala Advantage

Most European patients choose Kochi, Kerala for three reasons:

### 1. Ayurvedic Post-Surgery Recovery

Kerala is the birthplace of Ayurveda. After surgery, patients can spend 1–3 weeks at an Ayurvedic center for rehabilitation — accelerating recovery, reducing inflammation, and improving long-term outcomes. This combination of advanced surgery + holistic recovery simply doesn't exist in Europe.

### 2. English Proficiency

Kerala has a 95%+ literacy rate and English is widely spoken. From your surgeon to your nurse to your taxi driver, communication is seamless.

### 3. Direct Flights

Kochi International Airport (COK) has direct connections from London, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and major Gulf hubs — most European journeys require just one stop.

---

## What European Patients Say

*"I waited 14 months on the NHS for a hip replacement. Eventually I paid privately in the UK — £16,000. A friend told me about India. I had the same Stryker implant, the same quality of care, in Kochi for £4,500 — including flights and a week in a lovely hotel. I wish I'd done it sooner."* — David, 68, Manchester

*"IVF in London was £12,000 per cycle. We couldn't afford more than one. In India, we did two cycles — the second one worked — for less than one cycle in the UK. Our daughter is now 18 months old."* — Emma, 37, London

**Q: Is the quality of care really comparable to Europe?**
A: Yes. JCI accreditation ensures the same standards as top European and US hospitals.

**Q: How do I choose the right hospital?**
A: We match you based on your specific condition, budget, and preferences. All our partner hospitals are vetted.

**Q: What about follow-up care after I return to Europe?**
A: We provide a detailed medical summary and coordinate with your local GP or specialist. Virtual follow-ups are standard.

**Q: Is medical travel insurance available?**
A: Yes. Several international insurers now cover treatment in India. We can recommend options.

### How to Plan Your Medical Trip to India from Kenya: Complete Guide (2026)
Everything Kenyan patients need to plan a medical trip to India — visa process, flight booking, hospital selection, costs, recovery, and how Heal India handles every detail.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/medical-trip-india-from-kenya-guide
Category: Country Guides | Date: 2026-07-12 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

Over 3,900 Kenyan patients traveled to India for medical treatment in the first quarter of 2026 alone — making the Kenya–India corridor one of the busiest medical tourism routes in the world. And for good reason: a 7.5-hour direct flight, English-speaking doctors, JCI-accredited hospitals, and savings of 30–50% over private Kenyan hospitals for most major procedures.

Whether you need heart surgery, knee replacement, cancer treatment, or IVF, here's exactly how to plan your medical trip to India from Kenya.

---

## Why Kenyan Patients Choose India

### Cost Comparison: Kenya vs India

| Procedure | Kenya Private Hospital (KES) | India (KES equivalent) | Savings |
|---|---|---|---|
| Knee Replacement | 900,000–1,500,000 | 700,000–900,000 | 20–40% |
| Heart Bypass (CABG) | 1,500,000–2,500,000 | 1,000,000–1,400,000 | 30–50% |
| IVF (one cycle) | 600,000–900,000 | 400,000–600,000 | 25–45% |
| Kidney Transplant | 2,500,000+ | 1,400,000–2,000,000 | 30–45% |
| Cancer Surgery | 1,200,000–3,000,000 | 600,000–1,200,000 | 40–60% |

*Figures at March 2026 exchange rates. Travel costs (KES 200,000–350,000) included in calculation.*

### Beyond Cost: What Kenyan Patients Gain

- **Shorter wait times** — Surgery within days, not months
- **Higher surgical volumes** — Indian surgeons perform more procedures annually, translating to better outcomes
- **Premium implants** — Zimmer, Stryker, DePuy — the same brands used in European hospitals
- **Comprehensive packages** — Everything from airport pickup to post-discharge follow-up

---

## Step-by-Step: Planning Your Medical Trip

### Step 1: Initial Consultation

Send your medical reports via WhatsApp or email. A specialist from our partner network reviews them within 24 hours and provides a written treatment plan with transparent pricing.

**What to send:**
- Diagnostic reports (X-rays, MRI, CT scans, lab results)
- Doctor's referral letter
- Brief medical history (current medications, allergies, chronic conditions)

### Step 2: Treatment Plan & Hospital Selection

You receive:
- Recommended treatment options
- 2–3 hospital choices with itemized cost breakdowns
- Surgeon profiles and credentials
- Expected recovery timeline

Take your time comparing. We don't rush decisions.

### Step 3: Medical Visa Application

Kenya is on India's e-Medical Visa list. You apply entirely online.

| Requirement | Details |
|---|---|
| Processing time | 24–72 hours (apply 7 days before travel) |
| Cost | Approximately $80 USD |
| Validity | 60 days from arrival, triple entry |
| Documents needed | Passport (6+ months validity), digital photo, hospital invitation letter |

**Heal India provides your hospital invitation letter** — the most critical document — within 24–48 hours of treatment plan confirmation.

### Step 4: Book Flights

| Route | Airlines | Duration | Cost (Return) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Nairobi (NBO) → Kochi (COK) | Kenya Airways, Emirates, Ethiopian | 7.5 hrs direct or 9–12 hrs via Dubai/Addis | KES 80,000–150,000 |
| Nairobi (NBO) → Delhi (DEL) | Kenya Airways, Air India | 7.5 hrs direct | KES 85,000–160,000 |

**Tip:** Book 4–6 weeks in advance for the best fares. Mid-week flights (Tuesday–Thursday) are typically cheaper.

### Step 5: Arrive in India

Your coordinator meets you at the airport arrivals hall with a name board. Private transport takes you directly to the hospital or pre-arranged accommodation.

### Step 6: Treatment & Recovery

| Phase | Duration | Support |
|---|---|---|
| Pre-op workup | 1–2 days | Tests, surgeon consultation, anaesthetist review |
| Procedure | 1 day (or scheduled surgery) | Dedicated coordinator bedside |
| Hospital stay | 3–7 days | Private room, meals, nursing, physiotherapy |
| Recovery in India | 1–3 weeks | Serviced apartment, follow-up visits, WhatsApp support |
| Return home | — | Discharge summary, medication plan, remote follow-up |

---

## What Your Package Typically Includes

| Component | Included |
|---|---|
| Pre-operative diagnostic workup | ✅ |
| Surgeon, anaesthetist, and OT fees | ✅ |
| Implant/device cost (where applicable) | ✅ |
| Hospital stay in private room | ✅ |
| Nursing care and medications | ✅ |
| Physiotherapy/rehabilitation | ✅ |
| Airport pickup and transfers | ✅ |
| Coordinator support (24/7) | ✅ |
| Discharge summary and follow-up plan | ✅ |

**Budget separately:** Flights, visa fees, companion accommodation ($30–50/night), meals outside hospital.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How long does the medical visa take for Kenyans?**
A: Most applications are approved within 24–72 hours. We recommend applying at least 7 days before travel.

**Q: Can my spouse come with me?**
A: Yes. Spouse and children can apply for an e-Medical Attendant Visa.

**Q: Will there be a language barrier?**
A: All our partner hospitals have English-speaking medical staff. Swahili-speaking interpreters are available on request.

**Q: What if I have complications after returning to Kenya?**
A: We coordinate remote follow-up with your surgeon and local Kenyan doctor. Most hospitals offer WhatsApp-based post-operative support.

**Q: How do I pay for treatment?**
A: Bank transfer, credit card, or SWIFT payment. Most hospitals accept international payments.

### MS Treatment in India: Complete Cost Guide for International Patients (2026)
Compare Multiple Sclerosis treatment costs in India vs the US, UK & Europe. Stem cell therapy, Ayurvedic MS care in Kerala, and how to plan your treatment journey.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/ms-treatment-india-cost-guide
Category: Neurology | Date: 2026-07-10 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

Living with Multiple Sclerosis means facing not just the physical and emotional challenges of the condition, but also the financial reality of lifelong treatment. Disease-modifying therapies (DMTs) in the US can cost $60,000–$100,000 per year. In the UK, while NHS covers DMTs, access to advanced treatments like HSCT (hematopoietic stem cell transplant) or intensive rehabilitation is limited.

India offers a compelling alternative — not as a compromise, but as a pathway to affordable, world-class MS care that combines cutting-edge medical treatment with holistic Ayurvedic rehabilitation in Kerala.

---

## The Cost Picture: MS Treatment in India vs Global

### Disease-Modifying Therapies (DMTs)

| Therapy Type | US Cost/Year | UK Cost/Year | India Cost/Year |
|---|---|---|---|
| Interferon beta (Avonex, Rebif) | $60,000–$80,000 | NHS-covered | **$8,000–$14,000** |
| Ocrelizumab (Ocrevus) | $65,000 | NHS-covered | **$12,000–$18,000** |
| Natalizumab (Tysabri) | $70,000+ | NHS-covered | **$10,000–$16,000** |
| Fingolimod (Gilenya) | $60,000+ | NHS-covered | **$9,000–$15,000** |

### Stem Cell Therapy (HSCT)

| Country | Cost Range | What's Included |
|---|---|---|
| **India** | **$15,000–$30,000** | Full workup, stem cell harvest, chemo conditioning, transplant, hospital stay |
| United States | $100,000–$200,000 | Treatment only (excludes travel, accommodation) |
| United Kingdom | £40,000–£70,000 (private) | Limited NHS availability with strict criteria |
| Europe (Germany) | €50,000–€90,000 | Treatment + short hospital stay |

### Ayurvedic MS Care at Ayush Prana, Kerala

| Program | Duration | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| Inpatient MS Treatment (Ayush Prana) | 21–28 days | $3,500–$6,000 |
| Follow-up online consultation | Ongoing | $50–$100 per session |

---

## Why India Is Emerging as a Global MS Treatment Destination

### 1. Stem Cell Therapy at World-Class Standards

India's top hematology and neurology centers perform HSCT for MS using the same protocols as leading US and European hospitals. The procedure involves harvesting the patient's own stem cells, administering high-dose chemotherapy to reset the immune system, and reinfusing the stem cells to rebuild immunity.

Hospitals like Aster Medcity (Kochi), Apollo Hospitals (Chennai), and Max Healthcare (Delhi) have performed hundreds of HSCT procedures for MS patients from over 30 countries. Success rates — defined as no disease activity for 3+ years — range from 65–80% for relapsing-remitting MS, consistent with international benchmarks.

### 2. Ayurvedic MS Care — A Unique Differentiator

Kerala is home to **Ayush Prana**, the world's only Ayurvedic Multiple Sclerosis specialty hospital. Located on the banks of the Periyar River, 25 km from Kochi International Airport, Ayush Prana has treated MS patients exclusively since 2001.

Their approach combines:
- Custom herbal formulations prepared in an in-house GMP-certified pharmacy
- Personalized detoxification (Panchakarma) therapies
- Dietary and lifestyle modifications
- Yoga and meditation for neurological support
- Remote follow-up care after patients return home

Many patients combine conventional DMT or HSCT treatment with a 3–4 week Ayurvedic recovery program at Ayush Prana — a treatment model unique to Kerala.

### 3. Cost-Effective Long-Term Care

For relapsing-remitting MS patients who need ongoing DMTs, the cost savings in India are transformative. A year of Ocrevus in India costs approximately $15,000 compared to $65,000 in the US. Even after adding travel and accommodation, the total is typically 60–75% less.

---

## What Your MS Treatment Package in India Typically Includes

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| Initial neurologist consultation | MRI review, EDSS assessment, treatment plan |
| Disease-modifying therapy | As prescribed (Ocrevus, Tysabri, Gilenya, etc.) |
| Infusion administration | Day-care or short-stay admission |
| Laboratory monitoring | Blood work, liver function, infection screening |
| Rehabilitation | Physiotherapy, occupational therapy as needed |
| Follow-up care | Remote video consultations post-return |

**For HSCT packages:**
- Stem cell mobilization and harvesting
- Conditioning chemotherapy
- Stem cell reinfusion
- 2–4 weeks of inpatient monitoring
- Post-transplant vaccination schedule

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is stem cell therapy for MS approved in India?**
A: Yes. HSCT is performed at accredited hospitals under strict protocols for eligible patients with relapsing-remitting MS.

**Q: Can I combine Ayurvedic treatment with my DMT?**
A: Yes. Many patients receive both. Your neurologist and Ayurvedic physician coordinate the treatment plan.

**Q: How long do I need to stay in India for HSCT?**
A: Plan for 4–6 weeks total — 2 weeks for pre-transplant workup, 2–3 weeks for transplant and recovery, and 1 week for follow-up.

**Q: What is the success rate of HSCT for MS in India?**
A: Top Indian centers report 65–80% no-evidence-of-disease-activity (NEDA) at 3 years, matching international data.

**Q: Does insurance cover MS treatment in India?**
A: Some international insurance plans cover treatment in India. We can help verify your coverage.

---

## Your Journey with Heal India

1. **Share your medical history** — MRI reports, EDSS score, current treatment regimen
2. **Receive a personalized treatment plan** — recommendations for medical + Ayurvedic care
3. **Visa and travel arrangements** — medical visa invitation letter, flight booking
4. **Arrive in Kochi** — airport pickup, hospital admission, coordinator support
5. **Treatment and recovery** — with dedicated 24/7 support
6. **Return home** — detailed discharge summary, remote follow-up coordination

### Advanced Cancer Treatment in India: Navigating Oncology Care from Africa
A comprehensive guide on cancer treatment in India for African patients. Compare oncology costs, advanced therapies, and visa details.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/cancer-treatment-africa
Category: Oncology | Date: 2026-04-10 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I was diagnosed with stage 3 breast cancer in Lagos. The quote for treatment at a private hospital was ₦12 million — almost $15,000. I'm a teacher. I didn't have that kind of money, and honestly, I didn't know if I had that kind of time."

That's Grace, 44, from Lagos, Nigeria. Her story is not unique. Across Africa — from Lagos to Nairobi, Accra to Addis Ababa — thousands of patients face the same impossible equation: a cancer diagnosis that demands immediate treatment, but a local healthcare system that can't always deliver it affordably or quickly.

India treats over 800,000 international patients annually, with oncology being one of the top three treatment categories. For African patients, the combination of advanced technology, internationally trained oncologists, and costs that are 60–90% lower than Western countries makes India a compelling choice.

---

## How Much Does Cancer Treatment Cost in India?

| Treatment | US/UK Cost | India (Kochi) Cost | You Save |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cancer Surgery | $25,000 – $40,000 | **$4,500 – $7,000** | Up to 82% |
| Chemotherapy (per cycle) | $2,000 – $4,500 | **$400 – $800** | Up to 80% |
| Radiation Therapy | $15,000 – $25,000 | **$3,000 – $5,000** | Up to 80% |
| PET-CT Scan | $2,000 – $3,500 | **$180 – $250** | Up to 93% |
| Immunotherapy (per cycle) | $5,000 – $10,000 | **$1,200 – $2,500** | Up to 75% |

Even after adding flights from Lagos or Nairobi ($600–$1,000 round trip), accommodation for a month ($800–$1,200), and visa fees, the total cost is dramatically lower than treatment at home or in Western countries.

---

## What Advanced Cancer Treatments Are Available in India?

### Robotic Surgical Oncology
Indian cancer centres use the Da Vinci Xi robotic system for extreme precision in complex surgeries — prostate, lung, liver, pancreatic, and gynecologic cancers. Smaller incisions, less blood loss, faster recovery.

### Precision Radiation
- **CyberKnife** — Robotic radiosurgery for tumours in hard-to-reach areas
- **TrueBeam** — Image-guided radiation that adjusts to tumour movement during breathing
- **Proton Therapy** — Targeted radiation that spares healthy tissue (available at select centres)

### Immunotherapy & Targeted Therapy
India offers the latest FDA-approved monoclonal antibodies and immunotherapies — including PD-1 inhibitors like Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) — at a fraction of Western prices.

---

## Your Cancer Treatment Timeline in India

| Phase | Duration | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **Initial Consultation** | Day 1–2 | Virtual tumour board review of your reports. Free second opinion from senior oncologists. |
| **Arrival & Staging** | Day 3–5 | Hospital admission, PET-CT/biopsy, final staging, treatment plan confirmation. |
| **Treatment Phase** | Week 1–4 | Surgery, chemotherapy, or radiation therapy depending on your protocol. |
| **Recovery Period** | Week 2–6 | Post-operative care, nutritional support, physiotherapy as needed. |
| **Follow-up Plan** | Ongoing | Virtual follow-up with your Indian oncologist. Local doctor coordination in your home country. |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Can I get a second opinion on my cancer diagnosis before travelling?**
Yes. Send your biopsy reports, scans, and pathology slides to us via WhatsApp. We'll have them reviewed by a senior oncologist at our partner hospital within 48 hours — free of charge.

**How long do I need to stay in India for cancer treatment?**
It depends on your treatment protocol. Surgery patients typically need 3–4 weeks. Chemotherapy or radiation patients may need 4–8 weeks depending on the cycle. We'll help you plan the optimal duration.

**Can my family accompany me?**
Yes. India's medical visa allows two attendants per patient. Our partner hospitals offer family suites, and we can arrange furnished apartment rentals close to the hospital.

**Will my African medical insurance cover treatment in India?**
Some international health insurance policies cover treatment in India. We'll provide you with a detailed treatment plan and cost estimate that you can submit to your insurance provider for pre-approval.

**Is there post-treatment follow-up after I return home?**
Yes. Your Indian oncologist will schedule virtual follow-up consultations. We also coordinate with your local doctor in your home country to ensure continuity of care.

**What about language barriers?**
Our partner hospitals have international patient coordinators who speak English, French, Swahili, and Arabic. Translation services are available.

---

## How Heal India Guides You

1. **Free Virtual Consultation** — Send your medical reports via WhatsApp. Our oncology team reviews them within 24 hours and provides a treatment plan with transparent pricing.
2. **Medical Visa Assistance** — We provide the hospital letter and all documentation needed for your medical visa application.
3. **Arrival & Accommodation** — Airport pickup, hospital registration, and help finding furnished accommodation for you and your family.
4. **Clinical Coordination** — A dedicated coordinator is with you at every appointment, procedure, and follow-up.
5. **Long-Term Support** — Post-treatment follow-up care and coordination with your home-country doctors.

---

### Dental Tourism in Kerala: Combine World-Class Dental Implants with a Holiday
Save up to 70% on dental implants and full-mouth reconstructions in Kochi, Kerala. Learn about costs, dental clinics, and combining treatment with a holiday.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/dental-tourism-kerala
Category: Dental | Date: 2026-04-01 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I needed a full mouth restoration — all-on-4 implants on both arches. The quote in London was £28,000. I almost paid it. But a colleague told me about his experience in Kerala. I flew to Kochi, spent 10 days getting the same treatment with the same materials, and paid £6,500. The other £21,500 paid for my wife's holiday and a year of school fees."

That's Peter, 55, from London. His story is becoming increasingly familiar as patients from the UK, Europe, and the GCC discover that Kerala offers world-class dental care at a fraction of home-country prices — plus the bonus of a tropical holiday.

---

## How Much Does Dental Treatment Cost in India?

| Treatment | UK Cost | US Cost | **Kochi Cost** | You Save |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Single Dental Implant | $2,500 – $3,500 | $3,000 – $5,000 | **$600 – $900** | Up to 80% |
| Porcelain Veneer (per tooth) | $900 – $1,300 | $1,000 – $2,500 | **$250 – $350** | Up to 75% |
| All-on-4 Implants (full arch) | $14,000 – $18,000 | $20,000 – $30,000 | **$4,000 – $5,500** | Up to 75% |
| All-on-6 Implants (full arch) | $18,000 – $25,000 | $25,000 – $40,000 | **$5,500 – $7,500** | Up to 75% |
| Root Canal + Crown | $900 – $1,200 | $1,500 – $2,500 | **$150 – $250** | Up to 85% |
| Teeth Whitening | $500 – $800 | $600 – $1,200 | **$200 – $300** | Up to 65% |
| Smile Makeover (10 veneers) | $9,000 – $13,000 | $10,000 – $25,000 | **$2,500 – $3,500** | Up to 75% |

---

## Why Kerala for Dental Tourism?

### Advanced Dental Technology
Kerala's leading dental centres are equipped with:
- **3D CBCT scanning** — Complete jaw imaging for precise implant placement
- **Digital CAD/CAM labs** — Same-day crowns and veneers fabricated on-site
- **Laser dentistry** — Minimally invasive gum treatments with faster healing
- **FDA-approved implants** — Straumann, Nobel Biocare, Zimmer — the same brands used in Europe

### Fast Turnaround
Most dental procedures can be completed in 7–10 days. All-on-4 implants are often done in a single day, with the final fixed bridge fitted 3–5 days later.

### Holiday-Ready Recovery
Kerala's backwaters, beaches, and hill stations provide the perfect setting for the recovery period after dental surgery. Many patients find that a holiday mindset significantly reduces anxiety.

---

## Your Dental Tourism Itinerary

| Day | Activity |
|---|---|
| **Day 1** | Arrive in Kochi. Airport pickup, hotel check-in. Initial consultation and 3D scan at the dental centre. |
| **Day 2** | Implant placement surgery (usually 2–4 hours for full arch). Rest at hotel. |
| **Day 3–4** | Recovery at hotel. Soft food diet. Light sightseeing if comfortable. |
| **Day 5** | Follow-up appointment. Temporary prosthetics fitted. |
| **Day 6–7** | Explore Munnar tea plantations or Alleppey backwaters. |
| **Day 8** | Final fitting of permanent bridge or crowns. |
| **Day 9** | Polish and adjustments. Final check. |
| **Day 10** | Fly home with your new smile. |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What materials are used for implants and crowns in India?**
Our partner dental centres use the same FDA-approved materials as European clinics: Straumann and Nobel Biocare implants, zirconia and lithium disilicate crowns, and high-grade porcelain veneers.

**How do I know the quality is good?**
Kerala's top dental centres have international accreditation. Many dentists have trained or practised in the UK, US, or Australia. You can request virtual tours of the clinic and credentials of your dentist before travelling.

**What if I need follow-up care after returning home?**
Your dental centre will provide a complete treatment record, including implant serial numbers and post-care instructions. We can also coordinate with your local dentist for follow-up care.

**Is it safe to fly after dental implant surgery?**
For complex procedures, we recommend waiting 5–7 days before flying. Your dentist will clear you for travel. Light pressure changes during flight are normally fine after the first few days.

**Will I be in pain during the procedure?**
Dental implant surgery is performed under local anaesthesia. Most patients report less discomfort than a tooth extraction. Post-surgery pain is managed with medication and typically subsides within 48–72 hours.

**Can I combine dental treatment with Ayurveda?**
Yes. Kerala's Ayurveda centres offer therapies that complement dental recovery — herbal mouth rinses, anti-inflammatory massages, and stress-reducing treatments.

---

## How Heal India Facilitates Your Dental Trip

1. **Free virtual consultation** with your chosen dentist
2. **Treatment plan with itemised pricing** — no hidden fees
3. **Medical visa assistance** if needed
4. **Airport pickup & transfers** — hospital and hotel
5. **Accommodation booking** — near the dental clinic
6. **Itinerary planning** — sightseeing and recovery combined
7. **Local support** — 24/7 coordinator during your stay

---

### Joint Replacement Surgery in Kerala: Why Omani & GCC Patients Choose Kochi
Learn why patients from Oman and the Gulf choose Kochi, Kerala, for knee and hip replacement surgery. Compare costs, robotic surgery, and wellness packages.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/joint-replacement-oman-gcc
Category: Orthopedics | Date: 2026-03-20 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I couldn't kneel to pray. That was the moment I knew I needed surgery. For a Muslim, not being able to kneel for Salah is more than a physical limitation — it affects your spiritual life. My doctor in Muscat recommended knee replacement but quoted 11,000 OMR. I have six children. I couldn't justify that expense."

That's Ahmed, 68, from Muscat, Oman. His story is shared by thousands of GCC residents who are discovering that world-class joint replacement surgery in Kochi, Kerala — just a 4-hour flight from Muscat — offers the same quality as European hospitals at a fraction of the cost.

---

## How Much Does Joint Replacement Cost in India vs the GCC?

| Country | Knee Replacement (USD) | Hip Replacement (USD) |
|---|---|---|
| United States | $35,000 – $50,000 | $35,000 – $50,000 |
| United Kingdom | $18,000 – $25,000 | $18,000 – $25,000 |
| UAE (Dubai) | $15,000 – $22,000 | $15,000 – $22,000 |
| Saudi Arabia | $12,000 – $18,000 | $12,000 – $18,000 |
| Oman (Private) | $10,000 – $14,000 | $10,000 – $14,000 |
| **India (Kochi)** | **$4,000 – $6,000** | **$3,500 – $5,500** |

Even after adding a return flight from Muscat to Kochi ($250–$400), two weeks of hotel accommodation ($500–$800), and meals, the total cost is still **50–60% less** than surgery in Oman alone.

---

## Why GCC Patients Prefer Kochi

### 1. Closer Than You Think
Direct flights from Muscat to Kochi take just 3 hours 45 minutes. From Dubai, it's 3 hours 30 minutes. Many patients fly in on Sunday, have surgery on Tuesday, and are walking with support by Friday.

### 2. Robotic-Assisted Joint Replacement
Indian orthopaedic centres use the latest robotic systems — including MAKO and CORI — for knee and hip replacement. 3D modelling creates a patient-specific surgical plan, ensuring precise implant alignment that leads to:
- Faster recovery (walking same day)
- Less post-operative pain
- Longer implant life (20–25 years vs 15–20 with traditional methods)

### 3. Culturally Comfortable
- Arabic-speaking patient coordinators
- Halal meal options
- Multi-room family suites
- Prayer room facilities in hospital

---

## Your Joint Replacement Timeline

| Phase | Duration | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **Free Virtual Consultation** | Day 1 | Send your X-rays and reports. Orthopaedic surgeon reviews and recommends. |
| **Arrival in Kochi** | Day 2 | Airport pickup, hospital check-in, pre-surgery tests. |
| **Surgery** | Day 3 | Robotic-assisted joint replacement (1–2 hours). Walking with support within 6 hours. |
| **Hospital Recovery** | Day 4–7 | Physiotherapy begins day 1 post-surgery. Pain management, mobility training. |
| **Rehabilitation** | Week 2 | Continued physiotherapy at the hospital or nearby rehab centre. |
| **Ayurveda Recovery** | Week 3 | Optional post-surgery Ayurvedic therapies — herbal massages for stiffness, medicated steam for swelling. |
| **Return Home** | Day 10–14 | Most patients fly home 10–14 days after surgery. |
| **Virtual Follow-up** | 6 weeks | X-ray review and video consultation with your surgeon. |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Is the quality of implants the same as in Europe?**
Yes. Our partner hospitals use FDA-approved implants from the same global manufacturers (Zimmer Biomet, Stryker, Johnson & Johnson) used in European and American hospitals.

**Can I combine my treatment with Umrah?**
Many GCC patients combine their medical trip with a visit to Kerala's mosques and Islamic heritage sites. The 4-hour flight from Kochi to Kerala makes it convenient. We can help arrange this.

**How long will I need someone to care for me?**
You'll need assistance for the first 3–5 days after surgery. By day 7, most patients can walk with a walker independently. A family member or attendant is recommended for the first week.

**Will my GCC insurance cover treatment in India?**
Many insurance providers in Oman, UAE, and Saudi Arabia have international coverage that includes India. We'll provide the documentation you need for pre-approval. Contact your provider to check.

**Do you provide Arabic translators?**
Yes. Our partner hospitals have Arabic-speaking international patient coordinators available throughout your stay.

---

## The Kerala Advantage for GCC Patients

Kerala and the GCC have centuries-old trade and cultural connections. Today, that relationship extends to healthcare:

- Direct flights from Muscat, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha, and Riyadh to Kochi
- Familiar climate (Kochi is warm year-round)
- Wide availability of halal food
- Arabic-speaking communities and staff
- Ayurveda — a unique post-surgery recovery option unavailable anywhere else in the world

---

5. **Accommodation** — Hotel or serviced apartment for your family

### Affordable Cardiac Bypass Surgery in India: Quality Care for East African Patients
A complete guide on undergoing heart bypass surgery (CABG) in India. Compare costs, JCI cardiac hospitals, success rates, and medical visas from Africa.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/cardiac-bypass-surgery-africa
Category: Cardiac Surgery | Date: 2026-03-12 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

"By the time I was diagnosed, my arteries were 80% blocked. The cardiologist in Nairobi said I needed triple bypass. The quote at a private hospital was ₦12 million — and the public waiting list was 9 months. I didn't have 9 months."

That's James, 62, from Nairobi, Kenya. Like thousands of African patients each year, James found his answer not in Europe or America, but in Kochi, India — a city with some of the world's best cardiac care at prices that made his surgery possible.

---

## How Much Does Heart Bypass Surgery Cost in India vs Africa?

| Country | Average CABG Cost (USD) | What's Included |
|---|---|---|
| United States | $120,000 – $150,000 | Hospital stay, surgeon, ICU, medications |
| United Kingdom | $70,000 – $90,000 | Hospital stay, surgeon, ICU, medications |
| South Africa | $25,000 – $35,000 | Hospital stay, surgeon, ICU |
| Kenya (Private) | $10,000 – $15,000 | Hospital stay, surgeon — excludes ICU |
| Nigeria (Private) | $12,000 – $18,000 | Hospital stay, surgeon — excludes ICU |
| **India (Kochi)** | **$5,000 – $8,500** | **Full package: hospital, ICU, surgeon, meds, nursing** |

The cost in India is **all-inclusive** — no hidden fees, no surprise bills. A Kenyan patient flying to Kochi for bypass surgery typically spends under $11,000 total (surgery + flights + 3 weeks accommodation), compared to $15,000+ for surgery alone in Nairobi.

---

## Why African Patients Choose India for Heart Surgery

### World-Class Cardiac Surgeons
Indian cardiac surgeons are among the most experienced globally. Many have trained or practiced at leading US and UK institutions. A typical senior cardiac surgeon in Kochi has performed 5,000+ bypass procedures.

### JCI-Accredited Cardiac Centres
Our partner hospitals — Aster Medcity and Amrita Hospital in Kochi — are JCI-accredited, meeting the same international standards as the world's top hospitals. They feature dedicated cardiac wings with hybrid operating suites.

### Minimally Invasive Options (MICS CABG)
Traditional bypass requires splitting the breastbone and 7–10 days of hospital recovery. Minimally Invasive Cardiac Surgery (MICS CABG) allows surgeons to perform bypass through small incisions between the ribs — reducing hospital stay to 3–4 days and recovery time by half.

### Zero Waiting List
Your surgery can be scheduled within days of arrival. No waiting months for a slot.

---

## Your Heart Surgery Timeline

| Phase | Duration | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **Free Virtual Consultation** | Day 1 | Send your angiogram, echo reports, and medical history via WhatsApp. Cardiac team reviews within 24 hours. |
| **Arrival & Pre-Op** | Day 2–3 | Airport pickup, hospital admission, pre-surgery tests (blood work, ECG, chest X-ray). |
| **Surgery Day** | Day 4 | CABG procedure (3–6 hours depending on number of grafts). |
| **ICU Recovery** | Day 4–7 | 24–48 hours in ICU, then shift to private room. |
| **Hospital Recovery** | Day 7–12 | Physiotherapy begins, walking, medication stabilisation. |
| **Ayurveda Recovery** | Week 2–4 | Optional post-surgery Ayurveda therapies at Ayush Prana retreat — improves circulation and accelerates healing. |
| **Return Home** | Week 4 | Discharge with medication plan and follow-up schedule. |
| **Virtual Follow-up** | Monthly | Video consultations with your cardiac team. Reports sent digitally. |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**How safe is heart bypass surgery in Indian hospitals?**
JCI-accredited cardiac centres in Kochi report over 99% success rates for CABG procedures — comparable to top US and UK hospitals. Infection rates are lower than the international average.

**Can I bring a family member?**
Yes. India's medical visa allows two attendants. Your family can stay with you in the hospital and in nearby accommodation during recovery.

**What if I need emergency care after returning to Africa?**
Your Indian cardiac team provides virtual follow-up care. We also coordinate with your local cardiologist to ensure continuity. Your surgical records and imaging are provided in digital format.

**Is there a language barrier?**
Our partner hospitals have dedicated international patient coordinators who speak English, Swahili, and French. Translation services available for other African languages.

**How do I start the process?**
Simply send your medical reports on WhatsApp. Within 24 hours, you'll receive a detailed treatment plan with transparent pricing — completely free and no obligation.

---

## How Heal India Supports You

1. **Free cardiac consultation** — Send your reports, get a treatment plan within 24 hours
2. **Medical visa assistance** — We provide all hospital documentation
3. **Airport pickup & hospital check-in**
4. **Dedicated cardiac coordinator** — Available 24/7 during your stay
5. **Accommodation for family** — Hotel or serviced apartments near the hospital
6. **Virtual follow-up care** — Monthly check-ins after you return home

### Affordable IVF Treatment in India: A Complete Guide for UK & European Patients
Explore why UK & European patients choose IVF treatment in India. Learn about success rates, JCI hospitals in Kochi, cost comparisons, and legal guidelines.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/affordable-ivf-treatment-india-uk-europe
Category: Fertility | Date: 2026-03-05 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I was 38 when we started IVF in London. After two failed cycles and £18,000 spent, we had nothing to show except mounting debt and a broken spirit. A friend suggested we look at India. I laughed at first — but six months later, I was holding my daughter in Kochi."

That's Sarah, 40, from Manchester. Her story — of hope, heartbreak, and ultimately success — is becoming increasingly common as UK and European couples discover that world-class fertility treatment in India costs a fraction of what they'd pay at home.

---

## How Much Does IVF Cost in India vs the UK & Europe?

| Country | Average IVF Cost (USD) | Average IVF Cost (Local) |
|---|---|---|
| United Kingdom | $7,500 – $10,500 | £6,000 – £8,500 |
| Germany | $7,000 – $9,500 | €6,500 – €9,000 |
| France | $6,500 – $9,000 | €6,000 – €8,500 |
| Netherlands | $6,000 – $8,500 | €5,500 – €8,000 |
| **India (Kochi)** | **$2,000 – $3,000** | **₹1,70,000 – ₹2,50,000** |
| India (with ICSI/PGD) | **$3,000 – $4,500** | **₹2,50,000 – ₹3,80,000** |

Even after adding flights from London to Kochi ($600–$900), 14 days of accommodation ($600–$900), and meals, the total cost for a full IVF cycle in India is still **60–70% less than a single cycle in the UK**.

---

## Why European Patients Are Choosing India for IVF

### 1. No Waiting Lists
In the UK, NHS waiting times for IVF can stretch 12–18 months depending on your postcode and age. In India, you can begin your cycle within days of arrival.

### 2. Advanced Laboratory Technology
Indian fertility centres are equipped with:
- **ICSI** (Intracytoplasmic Sperm Injection) for male factor infertility
- **PGD/PGS** (Genetic Testing) for chromosomal abnormalities
- **Laser-Assisted Hatching** for improved implantation
- **Vitrification** — ultra-rapid freezing with 95%+ egg/survival rates

### 3. Higher Success Rates for Older Patients
For women over 38, Indian clinics report success rates of 35–45% per cycle with donor eggs — comparable to or better than UK average rates, at a fraction of the cost.

### 4. Surrogacy & Egg Donation Options
India has clear legal frameworks for both surrogacy and egg donation, with shorter waiting times than many European countries.

---

## Your IVF Journey Step-by-Step

| Phase | Duration | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **Free Virtual Consultation** | Day 1 | Send your medical history and reports. A fertility specialist reviews and recommends a treatment plan. |
| **Arrival & Initial Tests** | Day 2–3 | Blood work, ultrasound, semen analysis if applicable. |
| **Stimulation Phase** | Day 4–13 | Hormonal injections monitored via regular scans. 10–14 days typically. |
| **Egg Retrieval** | Day 14 | 20–30 minute procedure under light sedation. |
| **Embryo Transfer** | Day 17–19 | Fresh or frozen embryo transfer. Followed by 10 days of rest. |
| **Pregnancy Test** | Day 28–30 | Return to clinic for blood test. Virtual follow-up after returning home. |
| **Total Stay** | **14–21 days** | Most patients require 2–3 weeks in India. |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Are Indian fertility clinics as regulated as European ones?**
Yes. India's Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART) Act of 2021 regulates all fertility clinics. JCI-accredited centres follow international standards for laboratory protocols, infection control, and patient safety.

**Do I need a medical visa for IVF treatment in India?**
Yes. India offers a medical visa (M-visa) for fertility treatment. We provide all documentation including the hospital invitation letter. The visa is typically processed within 3–5 working days.

**Can I use an egg donor or surrogate in India?**
Yes. India allows anonymous and known egg donation. Surrogacy is legal for heterosexual married couples under the Surrogacy (Regulation) Act, with specific eligibility criteria.

**How many IVF cycles might I need?**
Success depends on age, diagnosis, and treatment protocol. Many clinics offer package pricing for multiple cycles. We recommend budgeting for 2–3 cycles maximum.

**What follow-up care is provided after I return to Europe?**
Your Indian fertility specialist will coordinate with your local gynaecologist and provide virtual follow-up consultations for the first trimester.

**Will my European health insurance cover IVF in India?**
Most European insurance does not cover IVF abroad, but the savings are so significant that even self-paying works out more affordable. Some international insurance plans do cover treatment — check with your provider.

---

## The Kerala Advantage

Beyond the medical treatment itself, Kerala offers a uniquely supportive environment for fertility patients:

- **Ayurveda therapies** — Stress-reducing treatments that improve uterine blood flow and hormonal balance
- **Nutritional support** — Customised meals for implantation and early pregnancy
- **Tropical setting** — Warm climate, fresh food, and serene backwaters create an ideal environment for the 2-week post-transfer waiting period

---

## How Heal India Supports You

1. **Free virtual consultation** with a senior fertility specialist
2. **Medical visa assistance** with documentation
3. **Airport pickup & hospital registration**
4. **Accommodation** near the clinic with kitchen facilities
5. **24/7 coordinator** throughout your stay
6. **Post-treatment virtual follow-up**

---

**📞 Ready to start your fertility journey?**

Get a free, no-obligation consultation within 24 hours.

📱 **WhatsApp:** [+91 80890 84080](https://wa.me/918089084080?text=Hello%2C%20I%20am%20inquiring%20about%20IVF%20treatment%20in%20India)
📧 **Email:** contact@santos.care

### Cosmetic Surgery in India: Cost, Safety & What International Patients Need to Know (2026)
What does cosmetic surgery actually cost in India vs the UK and USA? Rhinoplasty, liposuction, tummy tuck and facelift price ranges, safety checks, and how to plan your trip.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/cosmetic-surgery-cost-india-international-patients
Category: Cosmetic Surgery | Date: 2026-02-28 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I'd wanted a rhinoplasty since I was 16," says Chloe, a 29-year-old marketing manager from London. "But the quote in the UK was £6,500."

Chloe did what thousands of savvy patients are doing: she looked beyond her home country. The savings were dramatic, but more importantly, she found a surgeon with the right expertise at a facility that met international standards.

---

## The Real Cost Gap

| Procedure | India | UK (Private) | USA | UAE |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rhinoplasty | $1,200 – $2,500 | $5,000 – $9,000 | $5,000 – $15,000 | $6,000 – $10,000 |
| Liposuction | $1,000 – $3,000 | $2,500 – $7,500 | $2,000 – $8,000 | $4,000 – $8,000 |
| Tummy Tuck | $1,500 – $3,500 | $7,000 – $12,000 | $8,000 – $15,000 | $8,000 – $12,000 |
| Facelift | $2,000 – $4,000 | $5,000 – $13,000 | $7,000 – $15,000 | $7,000 – $12,000 |
| Breast Augmentation | $2,000 – $4,500 | $6,000 – $10,000 | $5,000 – $12,000 | $7,000 – $11,000 |

---

## How to Choose a Cosmetic Surgeon — The Right Way

1. **Board certification** — Indian Association of Plastic Surgeons (IAPS) or equivalent
2. **Portfolio review** — Before-and-after photos of similar cases
3. **Hospital accreditation** — NABH or JCI
4. **Clear communication** — Surgeon explains risks realistically
5. **Itemized quote** — Every charge broken down
6. **Patient reviews** — Real testimonials from international patients

---

## Realistic Recovery Timelines

| Procedure | Hospital Stay | Minimum Stay in India | Back to Work |
|---|---|---|---|
| Rhinoplasty | Day procedure or overnight | 10–14 days | 2 weeks |
| Liposuction | 1–2 days | 10–14 days | 1–2 weeks |
| Tummy Tuck | 2–4 days | 2–3 weeks | 3–4 weeks |
| Facelift | 1–2 days | 2 weeks | 2–3 weeks |
| Breast Augmentation | 1–2 days | 10–14 days | 2–3 weeks |

---

## The Safety Question

India's top cosmetic surgery facilities maintain rigorous standards:

- **NABH accreditation** — Indian equivalent of international quality standards
- **JCI accreditation** — Several partner hospitals hold this gold standard
- **Infection control** — Rates at accredited Indian hospitals comparable to Western benchmarks

---

## Your Journey with Heal India

1. **Share your goals and photos** on WhatsApp
2. **Receive surgeon recommendations** within 24–48 hours
3. **Virtual consultation** with the surgeon via video call
4. **We arrange your visa and travel**
5. **Surgery and recovery** with a dedicated coordinator
6. **Return home** with complete aftercare instructions

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How do I know the surgeon is qualified?**
A: We work only with board-certified plastic surgeons.

**Q: What if I need revision surgery?**
A: Reputable surgeons offer revision at reduced rates within a specified period.

**Q: Can I combine multiple procedures?**
A: Yes. Combining procedures is common and cost-effective.

**Q: How do I manage follow-up care after returning home?**
A: Your surgeon provides a detailed post-op plan with virtual follow-ups.

**Q: Is it safe to fly after surgery?**
A: Your surgeon clears you based on your recovery progress.

### India Medical Visa Guide for Kenyan & Tanzanian Patients (2026)
Everything Kenyan, Tanzanian & East African patients need for an India medical visa in 2026 — eligibility, documents, fees, and how Heal India handles the process.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/india-medical-visa-guide-kenya-tanzania
Category: Visas & Travel | Date: 2026-02-12 | Read time: 5 min | Author: Heal India Team

Grace, a 44-year-old mother of three from Nairobi, had been told she needed a hysterectomy. The quote at a private hospital in Nairobi: KES 1.2 million ($9,300). She didn't have that kind of money.

"Then my sister told me about a friend who'd gone to India for surgery. I thought it was impossible — I didn't even have a passport."

Two months later, Grace had her surgery at a JCI-accredited hospital in Kochi, paid less than half of what Nairobi quoted, and was back home recovering.

---

## First Things First: Are You Eligible?

Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda, Nigeria, Ghana, Rwanda, Ethiopia — all are on India's e-Medical Visa list. This means you can apply entirely online without visiting an embassy.

### e-Medical Visa at a Glance

| Detail | What You Need to Know |
|---|---|
| **Validity** | 60 days from first arrival in India |
| **Entries** | Triple entry (you can leave and re-enter) |
| **Processing time** | 24–72 hours (apply at least 4 days before travel) |
| **Cost** | Approximately $80 USD |
| **Companions** | Up to 2 family members can apply for e-Medical Attendant Visa |

---

## Step-by-Step: Getting Your Medical Visa

### Step 1: Get Your Hospital Invitation Letter

The hospital issues a formal letter confirming your name, passport number, proposed treatment, and admission date. **Heal India handles this for you.**

### Step 2: Gather Your Documents

| Document | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Passport | Valid for at least 6 months with 2 blank pages |
| Digital photo | White background, 2x2 inch, recent |
| Hospital invitation letter | Provided by Heal India |
| Medical reports | Referral letter from your doctor |
| Yellow fever certificate | Required if traveling from an endemic country |

### Step 3: Apply on the Official Portal

Visit **indianvisaonline.gov.in** and fill out the e-Medical Visa application form.

### Step 4: Book Your Flights

- From Nairobi: Ethiopian Airlines via Addis Ababa (6–8 hours total)
- From Dar es Salaam: Qatar Airways or Emirates (7–9 hours total)

### Step 5: Arrive in India

Present your e-Visa approval and hospital documents at immigration.

---

## What Happens After You Arrive

1. **Airport pickup** — Our representative meets you at arrivals
2. **Transport to hospital** — Private car
3. **Hospital registration** — We help with all paperwork
4. **Accommodation for companion** — Serviced apartment arranged in advance
5. **Full support** — A dedicated coordinator available 24/7

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Can my spouse travel with me?**
A: Yes. Spouse and children can apply for an e-Medical Attendant Visa.

**Q: Do I need a yellow fever vaccination?**
A: Yes, if you're arriving from a country with yellow fever risk.

**Q: What if my visa application is rejected?**
A: Rejections are rare with proper documentation. We'll help correct any issues.

**Q: Can I extend my visa if recovery takes longer?**
A: Yes. FRRO offices handle medical visa extensions with hospital documentation.

**Q: Do I need to speak English?**
A: Our partner hospitals have English-speaking staff. We also arrange Swahili-speaking interpreters.

---

## Start Your Journey

📱 **WhatsApp:** [+91 80890 84080](https://wa.me/918089084080?text=Hello%2C%20I%20am%20inquiring%20about%20medical%20visa%20from%20Africa)
📧 **Email:** [sales@santos.travel](mailto:sales@santos.travel)
🌐 **Free Consultation:** [santos.care/contact](https://santos.care/contact)

### Knee & Hip Replacement Surgery in India: A Cost Comparison Guide for UK, UAE & Gulf Patients (2026)
Compare knee and hip replacement costs in India vs the UK and UAE. Real price ranges, recovery timelines, and how to get a free treatment plan.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/knee-hip-replacement-cost-india-uk-uae
Category: Orthopedics | Date: 2026-02-05 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

Margaret is 67, a retired nurse from Dubai. For three years, she couldn't walk to the supermarket without stopping to rest. Her doctor recommended total knee replacement. The quote: AED 65,000 ($17,700).

"I sat in my car and cried," she says. "Not because of the surgery — I'd been expecting it. Because I didn't have that kind of money."

She found Heal India through a friend who'd had hip replacement in Kochi. Eight weeks later, she was walking along the beach in Kerala, pain-free, having paid less than half of what Dubai quoted.

---

## The Cost Picture — Honest Numbers

### Knee Replacement

| Country | Single Knee | Bilateral (Both Knees) |
|---|---|---|
| **India** (Kochi) | $3,500 – $7,500 | $6,500 – $12,000 |
| **United Kingdom** (Private) | $15,000 – $21,000 | $28,000 – $38,000 |
| **UAE / Dubai** | $17,000 – $22,000 | $32,000 – $40,000 |
| **United States** | Up to $49,000 | $60,000 – $80,000 |
| **Canada** (Private) | $15,000 – $25,000 | $28,000 – $42,000 |

### Hip Replacement

| Country | Single Hip | Bilateral |
|---|---|---|
| **India** (Kochi) | $3,000 – $7,200 | $5,500 – $11,000 |
| **UAE / Dubai** | ~$17,700 | $32,000 – $38,000 |
| **Germany** | Up to $30,000 | $50,000+ |
| **United Kingdom** (Private) | $15,000 – $20,000 | $28,000 – $36,000 |
| **Australia** (Private) | $18,000 – $24,000 | $32,000 – $42,000 |

---

## What Your Package Includes

| Item | Details |
|---|---|
| Pre-operative workup | Blood tests, ECG, X-rays, anaesthetist evaluation |
| Surgeon & anaesthetist fees | Entire surgical team covered |
| Implant cost | Branded implant of your choice (Zimmer, Stryker, DePuy) |
| Hospital stay | Private room with AC, TV, attached bathroom |
| Physiotherapy | Daily post-op physio included |
| Follow-up consultation | Surgeon review before discharge |

---

## Your Recovery Timeline

### Week 1 — Hospital Stay (Days 1–5)

Day of surgery: You'll be in recovery, then transferred to your room.
Day 1: Physiotherapy begins — sitting up, standing with support.
Day 2–3: Walking with a walker or crutches.
Day 4–5: Climbing stairs with support, discharged.

### Week 2–3 — Recovery in Kochi

You'll move to a serviced apartment. Daily physio visits continue. Most patients walk with a cane by end of week 2.

### Week 4 — Flying Home

Full recovery takes 6–12 weeks, but you'll be mobile long before that.

---

## Your Journey with Heal India

1. **Share your X-rays and medical history** on WhatsApp
2. **Receive a treatment plan** within 24 hours
3. **We arrange your visa invitation letter**
4. **Arrive in Kochi** — meet our coordinator at the airport
5. **Surgery and recovery** with daily support
6. **Return home** with full medical summary

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: How long before I can walk normally?**
A: Most patients walk with a cane within 2–3 weeks.

**Q: Will I need physiotherapy after returning home?**
A: Yes, and we'll provide a detailed rehab plan.

**Q: Can both knees be done in one trip?**
A: Yes. Bilateral replacement is common and more cost-effective.

**Q: What if I have diabetes or high blood pressure?**
A: Our partner hospitals routinely manage patients with controlled chronic conditions.

---

### IVF Treatment in India: Cost, Success Rates & What International Patients Need to Know (2026)
How much does IVF cost in India compared to the US, UK & UAE? Real success rate ranges by age, what's included in a cycle, and how to start your fertility journey.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/ivf-treatment-cost-india-international-patients
Category: Fertility | Date: 2026-01-22 | Read time: 6 min | Author: Heal India Team

"We'd been trying for four years," Sarah says, her voice steady but soft. "Four years of negative tests, of friends asking when we'd have children, of pretending we were okay when we weren't. By the end, IVF felt like our last hope — but the quote in London was £12,000 per cycle."

Sarah and her husband James are not unusual. Fertility treatment sits at a painful intersection: emotionally urgent, financially punishing, and time-sensitive.

They chose Kochi, India — not because it was the cheapest option, but because it gave them something the UK couldn't: **the ability to try multiple cycles without bankrupting themselves.**

---

## The Real Cost Comparison

| Country | Cost per IVF Cycle | Approx. 3 Cycles |
|---|---|---|
| **India** (Kochi) | $2,500 – $5,000 | $7,500 – $15,000 |
| **United Kingdom** (Private) | $8,000 – $12,000 | $24,000 – $36,000 |
| **United States** | $18,000 – $24,000 | $54,000 – $72,000 |
| **UAE / Dubai** | $10,000 – $16,000 | $30,000 – $48,000 |
| **Australia** (Private) | $9,000 – $14,000 | $27,000 – $42,000 |
| **Canada** (Private) | $8,000 – $15,000 | $24,000 – $45,000 |

---

## Realistic Success Rates — What the Data Actually Says

| Age Group | Per-Cycle Success Rate | Cumulative (3 Cycles) |
|---|---|---|
| Under 35 | 50–60% | 75–85% |
| 35–37 | 40–50% | 65–75% |
| 38–40 | 30–40% | 50–65% |
| 41–42 | 15–25% | 30–45% |
| Over 42 | 5–10% | 10–20% |

---

## Why Indian Fertility Clinics Match Global Standards

India's top fertility laboratories are world-class. Clinics in Kochi use **time-lapse embryo monitoring** (EmbryoScope), **pre-implantation genetic testing (PGT-A/PGT-M)**, **ICSI**, and **vitrification** — the same technology used in leading US and European clinics.

### The Speed Advantage

In the UK, NHS-funded IVF has waiting lists stretching 12–18 months. In India, you can begin your cycle within weeks of your initial consultation.

---

## What's Actually Included in an IVF Cycle Package

| Component | Included |
|---|---|
| Initial consultation & ultrasound | ✅ |
| Ovarian stimulation medications | ✅ |
| Blood work & hormone monitoring | ✅ |
| Egg retrieval procedure | ✅ |
| Lab fertilization (conventional ICSI) | ✅ |
| Embryo culture & monitoring | ✅ |
| Embryo transfer | ✅ |
| Embryo freezing (first year) | Usually included |

---

## What a Typical IVF Journey Looks Like

**Phase 1 — Preparation (at home, 2–4 weeks before travel)**
- Send your fertility workup via WhatsApp
- Receive a treatment plan and cost estimate within 24 hours
- Apply for e-Medical Visa

**Phase 2 — Treatment in Kochi (2–3 weeks)**
- Daily monitoring at the clinic
- Egg retrieval (day 14–16 of cycle)
- Embryo transfer (5–6 days after retrieval)

**Phase 3 — Recovery & Return**
- Rest for 3–5 days before flying home
- Remaining frozen embryos stored for future cycles

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Can I use donor eggs or sperm in India?**
A: Yes. India has regulated donor programs.

**Q: Is surrogacy legal in India?**
A: Yes, for heterosexual married couples.

**Q: How long do I need to stay in India for one cycle?**
A: Plan for 2–3 weeks total.

**Q: Will my embryos be safe frozen and shipped later?**
A: Yes. Vitrification technology has over 95% thaw survival rates.

**Q: What if the first cycle fails?**
A: Frozen embryos are ready for a FET cycle at lower cost.

---

### Heart Bypass Surgery Cost in India 2026: A Complete Guide for International Patients
Compare heart bypass (CABG) surgery costs in India vs the USA, UK & UAE. JCI-accredited hospitals, real savings, and how Heal India Medi Tourism plans your treatment in 24 hours.
URL: https://santos.care/blog/heart-bypass-surgery-cost-in-india
Category: Cardiac Surgery | Date: 2026-01-15 | Read time: 7 min | Author: Heal India Team

"I remember the exact moment the cardiologist said the word 'bypass'. My wife squeezed my hand so hard it hurt, but I barely felt it. All I could think was: how are we going to pay for this?"

That's John, a 58-year-old teacher from Manchester. He needed a triple coronary artery bypass. The private quote in the UK: £38,000. The NHS waiting list: 8 months minimum. "I didn't have 8 months," he says quietly.

John's story isn't unusual. Thousands of patients around the world hear the same words every day — and face the same impossible choice between debt and delay. But there's a third path that's helping more and more families, and it starts with a 10-hour flight to southern India.

---

## Why Patients Like John Are Choosing India for Bypass Surgery

### The Quality Question — Addressed First

Let's get the elephant out of the room: *Is the care as good?*

The short answer is yes — often better. India's top cardiac centers perform more bypass surgeries annually than most Western hospitals. **Aster Medcity in Kochi alone performs over 1,000 cardiac procedures a year.** That volume translates to experience. High-volume surgeons have better outcomes, period.

These hospitals hold **Joint Commission International (JCI) accreditation** — the same gold standard that top US hospitals carry. They follow identical protocols for infection control, ICU staffing, surgical checklists, and patient safety. The cardiac ICU at Amrita Hospital in Kochi has nurse-to-patient ratios that exceed UK standards.

"I was worried about quality," John admits. "But from the moment I walked into the hospital, I realized this wasn't a compromise — it was a world-class facility with doctors who'd trained at Harvard and Oxford."

### The Cost Reality

Here's what the numbers actually look like:

| Country | Typical CABG Cost (Self-Pay) | What You'd Save vs. India |
|---|---|---|
| **India** (Kochi) | $5,000 – $8,500 | — |
| **United Kingdom** (Private) | $25,000 – $55,000 | Save 75–85% |
| **United States** | $90,000 – $150,000 | Save 90–95% |
| **UAE / Gulf** | $30,000 – $50,000 | Save 80–85% |
| **Canada** (Private) | $30,000 – $60,000 | Save 80–85% |
| **Australia** (Private) | $35,000 – $50,000 | Save 75–85% |

Even after adding $1,500–$2,000 for flights, a week in a serviced apartment for a companion, and meals, the total comes to **$7,000–$10,500** — still a fraction of the cost in any Western country.

---

## What Actually Happens During a Bypass Surgery Package

### Before You Travel

Your journey begins not in a hospital, but on WhatsApp. You send your reports — angiography, ECG, echocardiogram — and a cardiologist from our partner network reviews them within 24 hours. If you're a suitable candidate, you receive a detailed written treatment plan with an itemized cost breakdown. No surprises. No hidden charges.

"We sent our reports on a Tuesday evening," John recalls. "By Wednesday afternoon, we had a full treatment plan, a cost estimate, and a suggested hospital. I couldn't believe how fast it was."

### What the Package Typically Includes

| Component | Details |
|---|---|
| Pre-operative diagnostics | ECG, blood work, chest X-ray, echocardiogram |
| Surgical team fees | Surgeon, anesthesiologist, perfusionist, OT staff |
| ICU stay | 1–2 days in cardiac ICU with continuous monitoring |
| Ward stay | 4–6 days in private room with AC, TV, attached bathroom |
| Medications | All standard post-op medication during hospital stay |
| Follow-up consultation | One pre-discharge review with your surgeon |
| Interpreter services | English-speaking staff (other languages available) |

### What You'll Need to Budget Separately

- Flights to Kochi International Airport (COK)
- Medical visa fees (~$80 per person)
- Serviced apartment for companion ($30–50/night)
- Meals and incidentals outside the hospital
- Extended stay if complications arise (rare, but worth planning for)

---

## A Week-by-Week Recovery Timeline

### Week 1 — Hospital (Days 1–7)

Day 1: Surgery day. You'll be in the cardiac ICU, waking up gradually. The team monitors your vitals continuously.

Day 2–3: You're moved from ICU to the ward. You'll be encouraged to sit up, then take a few steps with help. Pain is managed with medication.

Day 4–7: You're walking short distances, eating normally, and learning the breathing exercises you'll need at home.

### Week 2–3 — Recovery Stay in Kochi

Most international patients spend another 7–14 days in a serviced apartment near the hospital. Kochi's warm, humid climate is surprisingly good for recovery.

### Week 4 — Flying Home

Your surgeon clears you for air travel. You fly home with a detailed discharge summary, medication plan, and instructions for your local cardiologist.

---

## How to Choose the Right Hospital for Your Bypass Surgery

1. **Accreditation first** — JCI or NABH accreditation is non-negotiable
2. **Surgeon credentials** — Ask about their training, years of experience, and annual surgical volume
3. **ICU infrastructure** — A dedicated cardiac ICU with proper nurse-to-patient ratios (1:2 or better)
4. **International patient support** — Dedicated coordinator, interpreter services, airport pickup
5. **Package transparency** — Everything itemized in writing before you book flights

---

## Real Questions Patients Ask Us

**Q: How soon after surgery can I fly home?**
A: Most patients are cleared for air travel 2–3 weeks after surgery.

**Q: Can my spouse stay with me in the hospital?**
A: Yes. Private rooms allow one companion to stay.

**Q: What if there are complications?**
A: Complication rates at JCI-accredited Indian cardiac centers match international benchmarks.

**Q: Is the medical visa process complicated?**
A: No. We provide the hospital invitation letter, and you apply online for an e-Medical Visa.

**Q: Will there be a language barrier?**
A: All our partner hospitals have English-speaking medical staff.

---

1. **Send your medical reports** on WhatsApp or email
2. **Receive a written treatment plan** within 24 hours
3. **We handle your visa invitation letter** from the hospital
4. **Arrive at Kochi airport** — our representative meets you
5. **Full in-hospital support** — a dedicated coordinator
6. **Post-return follow-up** — we coordinate with your local cardiologist
